title: Nightlights vs Official GDP Kill-Test: does the autocracy over-reporting gradient reproduce on the VIIRS era?
description: An honest negative result. It tests whether Martinez's (2022, Journal of Political Economy) finding that autocracies overstate GDP growth, identified via a higher night-lights-to-GDP elasticity, reproduces on the VIIRS satellite era (2012-2023) using only free public data (World Bank WDI GDP, Earth Observation Atlas VIIRS sum-of-lights, V-Dem regime). Panel: ~170 countries x 2012-2023; model log(realGDP) = b*log(sum-of-lights) + g*[log(SoL) x autocracy] + country and year fixed effects, SE clustered by country. Verdict NO-GO: the clean within-country gradient g is +0.002 (insignificant, robust to dropping gas-flaring countries) and the cross-sectional gradient is -0.24 (significant but the wrong sign). The autocracy over-reporting signal does not reproduce on the short VIIRS window (Martinez's headline used DMSP 1992-2013), so a live VIIRS-era named-government discrepancy product has no reproducible signal. Code is MIT; the writeups are CC-BY-4.0. Reusable panel pipeline for a DMSP-bridge revisit.
